The commit message is:

You are looking at a version of Catch with customizable StringMakers for various types, including but not limited to: std::string, std::vector<>, std::array<>, std::optional<>, std::variant<>, and user-defined types. This allows users to control how these types are displayed in test output, providing more flexibility and readability. To enable a specific StringMaker, use the CATCH_CONFIG_ENABLE_[Type]_STRINGMAKER macro before including catch2/catch_test_macros.h.

true_message: Guard against CLR exceptions when translating exceptions
generated_message:
Compiling a mixed mode project with MSVC means that `CLR exceptions` will be caught in (...) as well. However, these do not fill-in `std::current_exception`, and thus lead to crash when attempting rethrow.

Avoiding this crash can be achieved by compiling the code with the /EHa switch enabled, which also causes structured exceptions to be caught in (...) but fill-in current exception properly. 
Now generate a commit message for this diff:
